LAW AND MOTION 612, RE-NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO COMPEL ROSALIA MALABANAN'S RESPONSES TO FORM INTERROGATORIES, SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES, SET ONE AND DEMAND FOR INSPECTION AND PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS - NO ORAL ARGUMENT AS NOTICE OF INTENT TO APPEAR NOT TIMELY GIVEN. (TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED) DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSES TO FORM INTERROGATORIES; SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES, SET ONE; AND DEMAND FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS, SET ONE, IS MOOT AND WITHDRAWN AS RESPONSES HAVE BEEN SERVED. THE COURT FINDS THAT THE LATE RESPONSES WERE A MISUSE OF THE DISCOVERY PROCESS. SANCTIONS ARE IMPOSED AGAINST PLAINTIFF IN THE AMOUNT OF $400, TO BE PAID WITHIN 30 DAYS OF NOTICE OF THIS ORDER. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT
NOTICE OF ENTRY OF ORDER/NOTICE OF RULING FILED FILED BY DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO

MOTION TO RECONSIDER ORDER GRANTING MTN FOR DISCOVERY SANCTIONS, PROOF OF SERVICE, POINTS AND AUTHORITIES, DECLARATION FILED BY PLAINTIFF MALABANAN, ROSALIA HEARING SET FOR NOV-12-2004 AT 09:00 AM IN DEPT 612

DISCOVERY, 612, PRESENT: FREDERICK CHRISTIAN ROESTI, ESQ. FOR PLAINTIFF/MOVING PARTY, ROSALIA MALABANAN. JILL L. GILLEN, DEPUTY CITY ATTORNEY FOR DEFENDANT/OPPOSING PARTY. MOTION ARGUED. COURT'S RULING AFTER HEARING: TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION OF ORDER GRANTING MOTION FOR DISCOVERY SANCTIONS IS DENIED. THE COURT DOES NOT FIND NEW LAW OR FACTS SUPPORTING RECONSIDERATION. C.C.P. SECTION 473 DOES NOT OFFER RELIEF TO THE IMPOSITION OF DISCOVERY SANCTIONS. THE COURT FINDS THAT THE MOTION WAS BROUGHT WITHOUT SUBSTANTIAL JUSTIFICATION. SANCTIONS ARE IMPOSED AGAINST PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL IN THE AMOUNT OF $800, TO BE PAID WITHIN 30 DAYS OF NOTICE OF THIS ORDER. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT

MINI MINUTES: Frederick C. Roesti, Esq., appearing for Plaintiff. Jill Gillen, Esq., appearing for Defendant and Moving Party.
Defendant City and County of San Francisco's Motion to continue Arbitration Hearing date and to Extend Arbitrator's Jurisdiction is heard this day. After argument of respective counsel court ordered motion granted. The Arbitrator's Jurisdiction is extended to 1/12/2005. The Arbitration OSC is re-set to 2/8/2005 at 1:30 PM, in Department 212.
